verbGlossary.FLAGGED DURING: Unsafe condition on {target} is identified and documented in a safety observation during active work (e.g., unsupported trench wall flagged during excavation)
verbGlossary.SOIL-TESTED BY: {source}of {target} are verified through a third-party or special inspection (e.g., bearing capacity of excavation bottom tested by independent lab)
verbGlossary.CAVE-IN REPORTED ON: {source} is formally recorded as a jobsite {target} (e.g., trench cave-in during excavation logged as an incident)
verbGlossary.DISCHARGE MONITORED VIA: Environmental effluent from {target}'s installation is tracked through a commissioning or environmental observation (e.g., dewatering discharge pH monitored via environmental observation)
verbGlossary.STORMWATER INSPECTED ON: {source}control measures on {target} are assessed through environmental observation (e.g., silt fence integrity inspected after storm event)
verbGlossary.COMPACTION VERIFIED IN: Soil or backfill density for {target} is confirmed through a material test report (e.g., backfill compaction at 95% Proctor verified in nuclear density test)
verbGlossary.LOAD-TESTED BY: {source} is confirmed through a third-party special {target} (e.g., pile load test performed by independent engineer)
verbGlossary.FOOTING CHECKED DURING: {source} placement are verified during a standard {target} prior to concrete pour (e.g., spread footing checked during pre-pour inspection)
verbGlossary.CRACK REPORTED ON: Structural deficiency in {target} is formally documented as a non-conformance report (e.g., horizontal crack in foundation wall reported as NCR)
verbGlossary.FLATNESS GAUGED ON: {source}of {target} is measured and recorded as an inspection item (e.g., slab-on-grade FF/FL numbers gauged on floor survey inspection item)
verbGlossary.SEAL EXAMINED ON: {source}of {target} is evaluated through a quality observation (e.g., below-grade membrane lap seals examined on quality walkthrough)
verbGlossary.CONCRETE SAMPLED FOR: {source} placed in {target} is tested via cylinder breaks documented in a material test report (e.g., grade beam concrete sampled for 28-day compressive strength)
verbGlossary.PRESSURE-TESTED PER: {source} for {target} is hydrostatically tested and results recorded in a system performance test report (e.g., water main pressure-tested per system performance test)
verbGlossary.SLOPE VERIFIED DURING: {source} grade for {target} is confirmed during a standard inspection (e.g., sanitary sewer slope verified during underground inspection)
verbGlossary.FLOW CAPACITY ASSESSED IN: {source}performance of the drainage element is evaluated in an environmental or commissioning {target} (e.g., storm drainage capacity assessed during commissioning observation)
verbGlossary.LEAK-TESTED BY: {source}of {target} is verified through a third-party special inspection (e.g., gas service piping leak-tested by independent inspector)
verbGlossary.ENERGIZED WORK CITED ON: Working on or near the energized element without proper safeguards results in a formal {target} (e.g., live electrical work without lockout/tagout cited on site electrical)
verbGlossary.WELD-INSPECTED BY: Structural welds on {target} are examined through a third-party special inspection (e.g., column moment connections weld-inspected by certified welding inspector)
verbGlossary.CAMBER CHECKED VIA: {source} deflection or pre-set camber is measured and noted in {target} (e.g., beam camber checked via quality observation before deck pour)
verbGlossary.SURFACE DEFECT LOGGED ON: Visible imperfection on {target} surface is recorded as a punch list item (e.g., slab honeycomb or crack logged as punch item on floor slab)
verbGlossary.BEARING VERIFIED ON: {source} is confirmed through an {target} item (e.g., roof truss bearing seat verified on framing inspection item)
verbGlossary.TORQUE DEVIATION FILED ON: Connection bolt tension on {target} falls outside specification and is recorded as an NCR (e.g., under-torqued high-strength bolts filed as non-conformance on steel connections)
verbGlossary.RISER VARIANCE NOTED ON: Dimensional inconsistency in {target} is documented in a deficiency notice (e.g., stair riser height exceeding code tolerance noted on deficiency notice)
verbGlossary.ALIGNMENT OBSERVED ON: {source}of the miscellaneous metal element is documented in {target} (e.g., embed plate alignment observed on quality walkthrough)
verbGlossary.WATER PENETRATION TESTED ON: {source}of {target} is validated through a system performance test (e.g., curtain wall water penetration tested per ASTM E1105 on mock-up)
verbGlossary.SCRATCH CATEGORIZED ON: Cosmetic damage to {target} is classified by issue type on a punch item (e.g., glass scratch on window categorized as manufacturing defect in punch item)
verbGlossary.HARDWARE SNAGGED ON: {source} hardware deficiency on {target} is captured as a punch item (e.g., panic bar misalignment snagged on exterior door punch item)
verbGlossary.MORTAR JOINT EXAMINED ON: {source} element is evaluated through {target} (e.g., mortar joint tooling and thickness examined on quality observation)
verbGlossary.ANCHOR CLASH RAISED ON: Physical interference involving {target}'s attachment is documented as a coordination issue (e.g., panel anchor conflicting with structural embed raised as coordination issue)
verbGlossary.CONTINUITY CHECKED ON: {source}coverage of the barrier element is verified as an {target} item (e.g., air barrier continuity at window-to-wall transition checked on inspection item)
verbGlossary.R-VALUE SHORTFALL NOTED ON: {source} element falls below specification and is documented in {target} notice (e.g., exterior insulation R-value shortfall noted on deficiency notice)
verbGlossary.FLOOD-TESTED PER: {source} element is validated through a controlled water test in a system performance test (e.g., roof membrane flood-tested per 48-hour ponding protocol)
verbGlossary.LAP DETAIL OBSERVED ON: Overlapping seam workmanship on {target} is documented in a quality observation (e.g., counter-flashing lap detail observed on quality observation)
verbGlossary.MOISTURE CONTENT TESTED IN: {source} element is measured and documented in a material test report (e.g., roof insulation moisture content tested via core sample)
verbGlossary.DRAINAGE CONFIRMED DURING: {source}performance of {target} is verified during a standard inspection (e.g., roof drain flow and slope confirmed during roofing inspection)
verbGlossary.CONDENSATION FLAGGED ON: Moisture accumulation on {target} is identified and recorded as a punch item (e.g., interior condensation on skylight glazing flagged as punch item)
verbGlossary.DUCT LEAK TEST SCHEDULED FOR: {source}test for the ductwork element is set up as a scheduled {target} (e.g., duct leak test scheduled for supply-air trunk on third floor)
verbGlossary.HANGER SPACING MEASURED ON: Support spacing for {target} is verified and recorded as an inspection item (e.g., HVAC pipe hanger spacing measured against spec on inspection item)
verbGlossary.AIRFLOW BALANCED PER: {source}delivery performance of the equipment element is validated in a system performance test (e.g., AHU supply and return airflow balanced per TAB report)
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONING RECORDED FOR: {source}performance of {target} is documented through a commissioning observation (e.g., boiler startup sequence commissioned and recorded for heating equipment)
verbGlossary.REFRIGERANT CHARGED PER: {source}levels in {target} are set and confirmed in a system performance test (e.g., chiller refrigerant charged per manufacturer startup requirements)
verbGlossary.SEQUENCE VALIDATED ON: Control logic for {target} is verified through a commissioning observation (e.g., economizer sequence of operations validated on HVAC controls commissioning)
verbGlossary.BALANCED AIRFLOW SCORED IN: Test-and-balance results for {target} contribute to the overall inspection pass rate metric (e.g., TAB diffuser readings scored in inspection pass rate)
verbGlossary.BACTERIA SAMPLED FOR: Potable water from {target} is tested for contamination in a material test report (e.g., domestic water system bacteria sampled for coliform test report)
verbGlossary.DRAIN CAMERA-SCOPED DURING: {source} is visually verified during a standard {target} (e.g., waste and vent piping camera-scoped during underground inspection)
verbGlossary.GAS LEAK CITED ON: Detected gas leak on {target} results in a formal safety violation (e.g., failed gas piping pressure test cited as safety violation on gas piping)
verbGlossary.FIXTURE DEFECT CATEGORIZED ON: {source} damage or malfunction is classified by issue type on {target} (e.g., chipped lavatory basin categorized as manufacturing defect on punch item)
verbGlossary.RELIEF VALVE SCHEDULED FOR: {source} on {target} is set for testing as a scheduled inspection (e.g., water heater T&P relief valve test scheduled for inspection)
verbGlossary.VAPOR BARRIER OBSERVED ON: {source} jacketing integrity on {target} is documented through a quality observation (e.g., pipe insulation vapor barrier overlap observed on quality observation)
verbGlossary.ARC FLASH ASSESSED BY: Electrical hazard severity at {target} is evaluated through a third-party special inspection (e.g., switchgear arc flash boundary assessed by independent engineer)
verbGlossary.WIRE FILL CHECKED ON: Conductor fill ratio in {target} is verified and recorded as an inspection item (e.g., branch circuit wire fill percentage checked on rough-in inspection item)
verbGlossary.BEND RADIUS OBSERVED ON: {source} routing geometry for {target} is documented in a quality observation (e.g., conduit bend radius observed on quality walkthrough for code compliance)
verbGlossary.FIXTURE AIM SNAGGED ON: {source} orientation or positioning issue is captured as {target} (e.g., recessed downlight aim offset snagged on lighting punch item)
verbGlossary.GENERATOR LOAD-BANKED PER: {source} is tested under simulated load in a system performance test (e.g., generator load-banked at 100% rated capacity per system performance test)
verbGlossary.RESISTANCE METERED IN: Ground resistance for {target} is measured and documented in a material test report (e.g., grounding electrode resistance metered in fall-of-potential test report)
verbGlossary.HYDRO-TESTED BY: {source} is pressure-tested through a third-party special {target} (e.g., sprinkler main hydrostatic test witnessed by fire marshal)
verbGlossary.COVERAGE VERIFIED ON: {source}of {target} is confirmed as an inspection item (e.g., sprinkler head coverage and clearance verified on fire protection inspection item)
verbGlossary.FLOW TEST SCHEDULED FOR: {source}test for {target} is set up as a scheduled inspection (e.g., standpipe flow test scheduled for annual fire protection inspection)
verbGlossary.CHURN TESTED PER: {source} element is validated in a system performance test (e.g., fire pump churn test run at zero discharge per NFPA 25 performance test)
verbGlossary.FALSE ALARM CORRECTED ON: {source}of {target} triggers a corrective action (e.g., fire alarm false alarm from dust during construction corrected via incident action)
verbGlossary.CABLE CERTIFIED IN: Structured cabling for {target} is tested and certified in a material test report (e.g., Cat6A data cable certified in channel test report)
verbGlossary.LOCKDOWN DRILL PLANNED FOR: Functional exercise for the security element is organized through an action plan (e.g., access control lockdown drill planned for security system commissioning)
verbGlossary.INTEGRATION VERIFIED ON: {source}of the building automation element is confirmed through a commissioning {target} (e.g., BAS integration with HVAC and lighting verified on commissioning observation)
verbGlossary.FINISH DEFECT CATEGORIZED ON: {source}issue on the interior element is classified by issue type on {target} (e.g., drywall nail pop categorized as workmanship defect on punch item)
verbGlossary.TOUCH-UP LISTED ON: Minor cosmetic repair needed on {target} is recorded as a punch item (e.g., paint touch-up at scuff mark listed on interior punch item)
verbGlossary.LIPPAGE OBSERVED ON: {source} edge height differential on {target} is documented in a quality observation (e.g., floor tile lippage exceeding 1/32 inch observed on quality observation)
verbGlossary.GRID SAG LOGGED ON: {source} deflection is recorded as {target} (e.g., acoustic ceiling grid sag at light fixture logged on punch item)
verbGlossary.CLOSER ADJUSTED VIA: {source} hardware on {target} is corrected through a punch item resolution (e.g., interior door closer swing speed adjusted via punch item resolution)
verbGlossary.SEAM DEFECT NOTICED ON: Visible joint imperfection on the flooring element is documented in a deficiency notice (e.g., carpet seam separation noticed on deficiency notice)
verbGlossary.DRAWER ALIGNMENT SNAGGED ON: {source} issue is captured as {target} (e.g., drawer front alignment and soft-close function snagged on casework punch item)
verbGlossary.CHIP DAMAGE FILED ON: Impact damage to {target} surface is formally documented as a non-conformance report (e.g., countertop edge chip filed as non-conformance against approved sample)
verbGlossary.PLACEMENT CORRECTED VIA: {source}of {target} is resolved through a punch item resolution (e.g., room signage placement height corrected via punch item resolution)
verbGlossary.ANNUAL CERT PERFORMED BY: Recurring code-required certification for the conveying element is conducted by a third-party inspector (e.g., elevator car annual safety certification performed by state inspector)
verbGlossary.ENTRAPMENT DOCUMENTED ON: Person trapped in or by {target} is formally recorded in an incident record (e.g., elevator entrapment event documented on incident record)
verbGlossary.CORE DENSITY TESTED IN: {source} material density is verified through destructive sampling in a material test report (e.g., asphalt core density tested in material test report)
verbGlossary.ADA SLOPE CITED ON: Accessibility gradient violation on {target} is documented in a deficiency notice (e.g., sidewalk cross-slope exceeding 2% cited on ADA deficiency notice)
verbGlossary.PERIMETER BREACH FLAGGED ON: Security gap at {target} is identified and documented in a safety observation (e.g., damaged perimeter fencing section flagged on safety observation)
verbGlossary.PHOTOMETRIC SHORTFALL LOGGED ON: {source}level below spec on {target} is recorded as a punch item (e.g., parking lot light fixture output shortfall logged on site lighting punch item)
verbGlossary.SITE RESTORATION TRACKED IN: {source} completion progress is monitored via {target} density metrics (e.g., landscaping punchout observation density tracked in closeout metrics)
